ArbOS 60 capacity headroom

Ben Berger — definition (Apr 30 sync)
Capacity = the gas-per-second throughput at which the price first starts rising above p_min. In ArbOS 51 it's a constant (10 Mgas/s post-DIA). In ArbOS 60 it depends on the workload mix. "On the second set the price will start going up when big G is about 15 Mgas/s — about a 50 % capacity gain on average."

Methodology

  1. Per-second aggregation. Bucket every block into 1 s windows (≈ 4 blocks each at Arbitrum's 0.25 s block time). For each second \(t\) define per-resource gas \(g_k(t)\), total gas \(G(t) = \sum_{{k}} g_k(t)\), and the workload mix \(\alpha_k(t) = g_k(t)\,/\,G(t)\).
  2. ArbOS 60 capacity (mix-dependent). The spec says, for every set \(i\) and constraint \(j\): \[\sum_{k} a_{i,k}\,g_k(t) \;\le\; T_{i,j}\] Substituting \(g_k = \alpha_k G\) and taking \(j = 0\) (the smallest \(T\) per set, which binds for sustained throughput): \[\text{capacity}_{60}(t) \;=\; \min_{i} \frac{T_{i,0}}{\sum_{k} a_{i,k}\,\alpha_k(t)}\]
  3. ArbOS 51 capacity (constant per regime). ArbOS 51 has the single inequality \(G(t) \le T_{51}\), so capacity is just \(T_{51}\): \[\text{capacity}_{51} = \begin{cases} 7 \text{ Mgas/s} & \text{pre-DIA} \\ 10 \text{ Mgas/s} & \text{post-DIA (Dia ladder, } j = 0) \end{cases}\]
  4. Headroom %. How much more gas/s the chain could absorb before \(p_k(t)\) moves above \(p_{\min}\): \[\text{headroom}(t) \;=\; \frac{\text{capacity}(t) - G(t)}{\text{capacity}(t)} \times 100 \%\] Plotted hourly as the mean of all active seconds.
  5. Saturation threshold. A second is "near saturation" when realised \(G(t)\) reaches \(\theta\) of the capacity ceiling (default \(\theta = 0.80\)): \[G(t) \;\ge\; \theta \cdot \text{capacity}(t)\] Panel 3 shows, per hour, the share of seconds that crossed this line.
  6. Top panel — simulated prices. Per-resource prices come straight from arbos60.Arbos60GasPricing.price_per_resource (same engine as the historical-sim chart): \[e_k(t) = \max_i\{a_{i,k}\,E_i(t)\}, \qquad p_k(t) = p_{\min} \cdot \exp\bigl(e_k(t)\bigr)\] with set exponents \(E_i(t) = \sum_j B_{i,j}(t)\,/\,(A_{i,j}\,T_{i,j})\) and backlog tick \(B_{i,j}(t{+}1) = \max(0,\; B_{i,j}(t) + \sum_k a_{i,k} g_k(t) - T_{i,j}\,\Delta t)\). Plotted as the gas-weighted hourly mean.
